Regression and sovereignty suites remain green, proving Rust-owned shaping is authoritative on this lane. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.
rows.problem: `client/runtime/systems/execution/task_decomposition_primitive.ts` still had large TypeScript fallback shapers for queue rows, dispatch rows, and summary math, preserving duplicated deterministic logic outside Rust when binary calls failed.
rows.title: Task decomposition queue/dispatch summary fallback removal (Rust fail-closed enforcement)

rows.acceptance: Route-task now treats Rust route primitives, habit matching, reflex matching, and complexity classification as authoritative by default and returns `MANUAL` with explicit `route_error` when Rust outputs are unavailable/invalid; optional TS fallback is explicit via `ROUTE_TASK_TS_FALLBACK=1`. Regression and sovereignty suites stay green. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.
rows.problem: `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` still silently fell back to TypeScript matching/classification when Rust outputs were unavailable, leaving duplicated deterministic routing logic active during outages.
rows.title: Route-task Rust authority hardening (fail-closed on Rust route primitive/match/reflex/complexity outages)

rows.acceptance: Added `execution_core route-evaluate` (`evaluate_route_json`) aggregating primitives + habit match + reflex match + complexity in Rust; `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` now consumes this aggregate result as the primary routing decision substrate; regression coverage includes `tests/client-memory-tools/route_evaluate_rust_parity.test.js` and fail-closed outage behavior in `tests/client-memory-tools/route_task_rust_failclosed.test.js`. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.
rows.problem: Route-task still orchestrated multiple separate Rust sub-calls (primitives/match/reflex/complexity), leaving aggregation/composition logic in TypeScript and increasing call-surface complexity.
rows.title: Route-task aggregate Rust evaluation command integration (`route-evaluate`)

rows.acceptance: Added `execution_core route-decision` (`evaluate_route_decision_json`) for deterministic route action policy evaluation; `route_task.ts` now consumes Rust decision outputs (with fail-closed `MANUAL` on decision command outage/inconsistency) while retaining JS-only command assembly. Regression coverage includes `tests/client-memory-tools/route_decision_rust_parity.test.js`, route fail-closed tests, and sovereignty gate suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.
rows.problem: `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts` still held branch-heavy action policy logic (reflex vs active/candidate/manual/autocrystallize), leaving final route decision matrix in TypeScript after Rust evaluation.
rows.title: Route-task action policy migration into Rust core (`route-decision`)

rows.acceptance: Removed dead TS fallback helper functions and exported no legacy route-normalization shim; live route behavior remains Rust-authoritative via `route-evaluate` + `route-decision` with fail-closed handling. Regression coverage includes route fail-closed, budget guard, emergency stop, directive gate, and execution sovereignty suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.
rows.problem: After `route-evaluate` + `route-decision` cutover, `route_task.ts` still contained large unused TS fallback helpers for primitives/match/reflex/complexity, increasing TS surface area and risking accidental reactivation.
rows.title: Route-task legacy TS route fallback removal (post Rust cutover)

rows.acceptance: Added `execution_core route-habit-readiness` (`evaluate_route_habit_readiness_json`) and wired `route_task.ts` to consume it fail-closed before `route-decision`; regression coverage includes `tests/client-memory-tools/route_habit_readiness_rust_parity.test.js`, route fail-closed checks, and execution sovereignty suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.
rows.problem: `route_task.ts` still computed habit readiness details in TypeScript (required inputs parsing, trusted entrypoint check, state normalization), leaving match gating partially outside Rust control.
rows.title: Route-task habit readiness migration into Rust core (`route-habit-readiness`)

rows.acceptance: Removed local JS fallback gate evaluation paths; constitution gate now fail-closes to `DENY` and heroic gate fail-closes to blocked when Rust gate outputs are unavailable. Regression + sovereignty suites validate behavior under normal Rust authority and preserve fail-closed posture. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.
rows.problem: `client/runtime/systems/execution/task_decomposition_primitive.ts` still used local JS fallback evaluations for constitution and heroic gates when Rust gate commands were unavailable, leaving core sovereignty decisions partially outside Rust authority.
rows.title: Task decomposition constitution/heroic gate fallback removal (Rust fail-closed authority)

rows.acceptance: `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts` now separates bootstrap context from dynamic memory context, enforces configurable pre-invocation context budget (default `2000`) with trim/reject modes, logs over-budget violations to `personas/organization/telemetry.jsonl` and persona correspondence, and emits structured `context_budget` metadata. Loop hooks now pass explicit budget flags in `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/inversion_controller.ts` and `client/cognition/adaptive/rsi/rsi_bootstrap.ts`. Regression + sovereignty checks: `tests/client-memory-tools/personas_context_budget_guard.test.js`, `tests/client-memory-tools/personas_lens_cli.test.js`, `tests/client-memory-tools/execution_security_gate_integration.test.js`.
rows.problem: Persona lens invocations could over-inject dynamic memory context, causing token waste/drift and inconsistent runtime cost in single/multi/triggered loop calls.
rows.title: Personas bootstrap vs dynamic memory split + token budget guard

rows.acceptance: Added `protheus setup` (`client/runtime/systems/ops/protheus_setup_wizard.ts`) with covenant confirmation, interaction style (proactive/silent), notification preference, and skippable operation (`--skip-setup` / `PROTHEUS_SKIP_SETUP=1`). Dispatcher now auto-offers setup before REPL on first TTY run (`core/layer0/ops/src/protheusctl.rs`). Command index/docs updated and regression coverage added in `tests/client-memory-tools/protheus_setup_wizard.test.js` plus list/repl guard tests.
rows.problem: New users lacked a short covenant-first setup flow, making first-run discovery rely on manual command memory.

rows.acceptance: Coordinator implementation partitions work, deduplicates findings (same item_id), merges severity ratings, and emits unified report; authority is `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrapper `client/cognition/orchestration/coordinator.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/coordinator.test.js` and `core/layer0/ops/tests/orchestration_domain_integration.rs`.
rows.problem: Parallel agents produce overlapping findings without a coordinator to partition work and deduplicate results.
rows.title: Coordinator Agent Pattern

rows.acceptance: Shared scratchpad at `local/workspace/scratchpad/{task_id}.json` supports read/write of progress, findings, checkpoints; schema versioned; cleaned up on completion; authority is `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrapper `client/cognition/orchestration/scratchpad.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/scratchpad.test.js` and `core/layer0/ops/tests/orchestration_domain_integration.rs`.
rows.problem: Agents work in isolation and cannot build on each other's discoveries without a shared workspace.
rows.title: Shared State / Scratchpad

rows.acceptance: Agents write checkpoint every 10 items or 2 minutes; on timeout, return partial results + checkpoint location; one automatic retry; authority is `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrapper `client/cognition/orchestration/checkpoint.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/checkpoint.test.js`.
rows.problem: Long-running agents can timeout without returning partial results, losing all progress.
rows.title: Checkpointing and Timeout Recovery

rows.acceptance: Scope format supports `series:[]` and `paths:[]`; coordinator validates non-overlap before spawning; scope violations logged separately; authority is `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrapper `client/cognition/orchestration/scope.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/scope.*.test.js`.
rows.problem: Overlapping work assignments waste resources and produce duplicate findings.
rows.title: Strict Scope Boundaries

rows.acceptance: Schema defined in `client/cognition/orchestration/schemas/finding-v1.json` with required fields (audit_id, item_id, severity, status, location, evidence, timestamp); severity/status enums enforced in core authority `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrapper `client/cognition/orchestration/schema_runtime.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/schema.test.js`.
rows.problem: Agents return findings in inconsistent formats, making aggregation difficult.
rows.title: Standardized Output Schema

rows.acceptance: Task group ID assigned at spawn; system tracks agent status; auto-notify parent when all done/failed/timeout; include counts in notification; authority is `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrappers `client/cognition/orchestration/completion.ts` and `client/cognition/orchestration/taskgroup.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/completion.*.test.js`.
rows.problem: Parent session must manually poll to detect when all subagents complete.
rows.title: Completion Triggers

rows.acceptance: Task group ID format `{task_type}-{timestamp}-{nonce}`; all subagents tagged; API supports group querying; metadata includes created_at, coordinator_session, agent_count, status; authority is `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrapper `client/cognition/orchestration/taskgroup.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/taskgroup.*.test.js`.
rows.problem: No way to query all agents in a task group or check collective status.
rows.title: Task Group Metadata

rows.acceptance: Session-history partial retrieval plus checkpoint fallback supports retry/continue/abort decisions; authority is `core/layer0/ops/src/orchestration.rs` with client wrapper `client/cognition/orchestration/partial.ts`; validated by `tests/client/cognition/partial.*.test.js`.
rows.problem: Timed-out agents lose all findings with no way to recover partial results.
rows.title: Partial Result Retrieval

rows.acceptance: `protheusd subscribe` now chunks long waits and, on bridge timeout/gate conditions, drains deterministic local compat attention state (`queue.jsonl` + per-consumer cursors) to emit non-degraded batches (`bridge_fallback_local=true`) instead of timeout-degraded envelopes for idle/no-event windows. rows.acceptance: `protheusd subscribe` now defaults to native push transport (`--transport=push`) with mutation-driven wake (`waitForAttentionPush`), bounded heartbeat wait windows, preserved cursor/ack semantics via `attention-queue drain`, and explicit push metadata in receipts (`transport`, `native_push`, `push_wait_reason`). Timeout floors were tuned for push mode (`bridge` 8s / `stdio` 12s defaults) to fail fast into deterministic fallback instead of long blocking windows.

rows.problem: Portable deploy units need a strict manifest contract for capability/budget/provenance correctness.
rows.title: `CellBundle` manifest schema + validator lane

rows.acceptance: Delivered world registry contract (`planes/contracts/wit/world_registry_v1.json`) and compatibility validation lane (`protheus-ops metakernel worlds --strict=1`) that validates manifest-declared worlds/capabilities against registry; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-004:run`.
rows.problem: Pluggable substrate execution requires a stable component boundary independent of TS wrappers.
rows.title: WIT world registry + wasm component ABI contract

rows.acceptance: Delivered capability taxonomy policy (`planes/contracts/capability_effect_taxonomy_v1.json`) and strict gating lane (`protheus-ops metakernel capability-taxonomy --strict=1`) for effect/risk derivation with high-risk policy-gate checks + receipts; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-005:run`.
rows.problem: Capability safety depends on typed effects and deterministic risk-tier handling rules.
rows.title: Effect-typed capability taxonomy + risk class policy defaults (R0-R4)

rows.acceptance: Delivered admission policy (`planes/contracts/budget_admission_policy_v1.json`) and fail-closed admission lane (`protheus-ops metakernel budget-admission --strict=1`) that emits bounded `reason_codes` on denial; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-006:run`.
rows.problem: Budgets must bind before execution to prevent post-hoc overrun behavior.
rows.title: Budget admission controller across CPU/RAM/storage/network/tokens/power/privacy/cognitive-load

rows.acceptance: Delivered `planes/contracts/epistemic_object_v1.schema.json` + example object and strict metakernel validator lane (`protheus-ops metakernel epistemic-object --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-007:run`.
rows.problem: AI-native state needs typed uncertainty and lineage metadata, not byte-only storage semantics.
rows.title: Epistemic object schema v1 (value/schema/provenance/confidence/policy/retention/export/rollback)

rows.acceptance: Delivered effect-journal policy + example (`planes/contracts/effect_journal_policy_v1.json`, `planes/contracts/examples/effect_journal.minimal.json`) and strict enforcement lane (`protheus-ops metakernel effect-journal --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-008:run`.
rows.problem: External side effects must be journaled deterministically to preserve replay and audit guarantees.
rows.title: Effect journal commit-before-actuate enforcement

rows.acceptance: Delivered substrate descriptor registry (`planes/contracts/substrate_descriptor_registry_v1.json`) with required descriptor fields + degrade matrix checks and strict validator lane; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-009:run`.
rows.problem: Portable execution across MCU/edge/cloud/quantum/neural nodes requires machine-readable substrate contracts.
rows.title: Substrate descriptor registry + degrade matrix

rows.acceptance: Delivered radix guard policy (`planes/contracts/radix_policy_guard_v1.json`) and strict guard lane (`protheus-ops metakernel radix-guard --strict=1`) enforcing binary-required critical paths + ternary overlap rejection; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-010:run`.
rows.problem: Radix experimentation must not weaken core security invariants.
rows.title: Radix policy guard (binary baseline, ternary optional class-scoped lowering)

rows.acceptance: Delivered `planes/contracts/quantum_broker_domain_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel quantum-broker --strict=1` validating required operations + classical fallback receipt policy; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-011:run`.
rows.problem: Quantum execution is hybrid and queue-bound; it needs explicit broker semantics and fallback guarantees.
rows.title: Quantum broker domain contract (`compile/estimate/submit/session/batch/measure`)

rows.acceptance: Delivered `planes/contracts/neural_consent_kernel_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel neural-consent --strict=1` enforcing separated authorities and dual-control/consent/audit stimulate policy; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-012:run`.
rows.problem: Neural interfaces require strict authority boundaries and highest-risk controls for stimulation paths.
rows.title: Neural consent kernel scaffold with separated authorities (`observe/infer/feedback/stimulate`)

rows.acceptance: Delivered `planes/contracts/attestation_graph_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel attestation-graph --strict=1` validating domain linkage across code/model/policy/data/effect edges; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-013:run`.
rows.problem: Runtime trust requires signed lineage across artifacts, not isolated per-lane proofs.
rows.title: Attestation graph service for code/model/policy/data/effect lineage

rows.acceptance: Delivered `planes/contracts/degradation_contracts_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel degradation-contracts --strict=1` enforcing per-critical-lane fallback declarations with no privilege widening; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-014:run`.
rows.problem: Degraded operation must preserve safety while staying operational within declared fallbacks.
rows.title: Degradation-contract verifier (`no-network/no-ternary/no-qpu/neural-link-loss`)

rows.acceptance: Delivered `planes/contracts/execution_profile_matrix_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel execution-profiles --strict=1` verifying required profile IDs and harness declarations; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-015:run`.
rows.problem: Profile claims need reproducible execution evidence, not architecture-only assertions.
rows.title: Execution profile matrix harness (`mcu`, `edge`, `cloud`)

rows.acceptance: Human-approved provider access is active (`HMAN-084`) and signed live QPU run artifacts satisfy fidelity/latency policy thresholds.
rows.problem: Quantum broker claims require real provider execution evidence and credentialed access.
rows.title: Live QPU provider validation campaign

rows.acceptance: Human-approved hardware access (`HMAN-081`) plus benchmark artifact bundle shows class-scoped gains and no safety-policy violations.
rows.problem: Ternary lowering claims need real hardware proof before production promotion.
rows.title: Ternary hardware lowering validation campaign

rows.acceptance: Human approvals (`HMAN-082`, `HMAN-083`) and device-access evidence complete staged safety validation before lane promotion.
rows.problem: Neural interface paths require ethics/legal/hardware governance not executable from autonomous lanes.
rows.title: Neural I/O safety validation campaign

rows.acceptance: Machine-checked Lean proof pack added at `proofs/layer0/Layer0Invariants.lean`, verified by `proofs/layer0/verify.sh`, with deterministic artifact receipt `core/local/artifacts/layer0_invariant_proof_pack.json`; `verify.sh` root gate now executes this proof step.
rows.problem: Invariants are documented and partially enforced, but not fully machine-checked end-to-end as a single trust artifact.
rows.title: Layer-0 machine-checked invariant proof pack (Lean/Coq/Rocq)

rows.acceptance: CI proof execution is now enforced: `.github/workflows/formal-proof-runtime.yml` installs `kani-verifier` + runs `cargo kani setup`, and `package.json` runs `ops:formal-proof:run` with `--check-toolchains=1 --execute-proofs=1 --execute-optional-proofs=1`; required Kani command is pinned in `proofs/layer0/core_formal_coverage_map.json` (`kani_top1_proofs`) against `core/layer0/ops/src/top1_kani_proofs.rs`. Promotion to `done` remains pending first green CI proof receipt due local runner instability with long `cargo kani` session capture.
rows.problem: Runtime hardening still relies heavily on tests/guards rather than broad property proofs across core crates.
rows.title: Core Rust formal proof coverage lane (Kani/Prusti)

rows.acceptance: Extend CI with nightly proof re-run gate and bind `verify.sh` to proof/artifact verification so proof drift fails closed.
rows.problem: Proof posture can drift without continuous re-verification across new commits.
rows.title: Nightly full-kernel re-proof CI + `verify.sh` proof gate

rows.acceptance: Publish reproducible VM/container artifact that replays proof verification deterministically and emits signed verification receipts.
rows.problem: External trust requires independent replayability of proof verification, not internal-only receipts.
rows.title: Reproducible public proof VM artifact lane

rows.acceptance: Strict static-musl size gate passes via `cargo run --manifest-path core/layer0/ops/Cargo.toml --bin protheus-ops -- top1-assurance size-gate --strict=1` (receipt hash `7c8f36e3a446eb80fdfef2aeb08d3e3cc54aeeeb9edae914aa11c360bdc85b4c`, static-pie detected, `size_mb=0.528`, `max_mb=35.0`); CI gate in `.github/workflows/protheusd-static-size-gate.yml` enforces static build and max-band failure.
rows.problem: Runtime still depends on distributed wrappers/host assumptions that weaken deployment simplicity and assurance claims.
rows.title: Single static `protheusd` binary profile (25-35MB target)

rows.acceptance: Deterministic rebuild equivalence now enforced and evidenced: two consecutive static-musl builds produced identical SHA-256 (`762c260ee95b1c112c1ade60ca4f9c09b846cad4794106559535c87286e624bd`) with receipt artifact `core/local/state/ops/top1_assurance/reproducible_build_latest.json`; release lane publishes `state/release/provenance_bundle/reproducible_build_equivalence.json` and references it from provenance bundle metadata.
rows.problem: Production-depth deployment needs deterministic artifact lineage and independent reproducibility.
rows.title: Single-binary reproducible supply-chain release contract

rows.acceptance: Strict benchmark refresh passes via `cargo run --manifest-path core/layer0/ops/Cargo.toml --bin protheus-ops -- top1-assurance benchmark-thresholds --strict=1 --refresh=1 --sample-ms=800` (receipt hash `e1bc9458decdbff9ada49b6158b82bd88170c63ea586b1cebad46d4a461d9e7c`; metrics: `cold_start_ms=74.5`, `idle_rss_mb=22.1`, `tasks_per_sec=7420`); CI uploads `core/local/state/ops/top1_assurance/benchmark_latest.json` for regression gating.
rows.problem: Comparative claims need reproducible, policy-bound performance evidence.
rows.title: Public benchmark harness with strict performance thresholds

rows.acceptance: Add CI-updated comparison table from canonical benchmark artifacts with deterministic generation script and evidence links.
rows.problem: Comparative positioning drifts without continuously regenerated side-by-side evidence.
rows.title: Public "Protheus vs X" CI-updated comparison matrix

rows.acceptance: Human-contracted external verification is completed and published with linked findings/remediation evidence (`HMAN-087`).
rows.problem: External assurance requires non-self-asserted verification from respected third parties.
rows.title: Independent third-party verification publication lane

rows.acceptance: Human-approved high-assurance profile scope and claim boundaries are published, and profile artifacts pass policy + proof + benchmark gates (`HMAN-086`).
rows.problem: High-stakes profile claims require governance/legal scope approval beyond technical implementation.
rows.title: High-assurance profile release gate (medical/defense-class)

rows.acceptance: Add nightly model-check + symbolic/proof coverage gate for Layer-0/1 critical paths; CI fails closed if coverage drops below policy floor or any sacred invariant proof artifact is missing, with deterministic coverage receipts attached per run.
rows.problem: Proof suites run, but there is no explicit nightly coverage floor proving 100% covenant/fail-closed path inclusion for sacred core invariants.
rows.title: Sacred-Core Nightly Proof Coverage Floor Contract

rows.acceptance: Add `protheus self-host verify` flow where shadow operators execute full source bootstrap/build/verify pipeline (Rust core + client runtime), emitting deterministic stage receipts and fail-closed rollback when bootstrap parity gates fail.
rows.problem: Self-hosting capability exists, but there is no recurring proof that Protheus can rebuild and validate its own runtime end-to-end through governed agent orchestration.
rows.title: Autonomous Self-Hosting Build Receipt Contract

rows.acceptance: Add inversion-gated self-mod loop that proposes skill/workflow/scheduling primitive changes, validates invariants in sandbox, deploys with deterministic receipts, and auto-rolls back on policy/regression breach.
rows.problem: RSI stalls without a governed pipeline that can propose, simulate, merge, and rollback self-authored architectural changes.
rows.title: True Recursive Self-Modification Loop Contract

rows.acceptance: Run persistent metacognitive reflection loop with goal-drift detection, exploration drive under idle states, and strategy-adjustment memory artifacts that emit deterministic explainability receipts.
rows.problem: System agency remains reactive unless reflective loops can detect drift, create sub-goals, and self-correct continuously.
rows.title: Emergent Metacognition + Will Layer Contract

rows.acceptance: Enable spontaneous sub-swarm formation and RL-improvement sharing with receipted convergence/health metrics and policy-bounded resource allocation/reinvestment flows.
rows.problem: RSI cannot compound at network scale without coordination-free learning share, value accrual, and visible health telemetry.
rows.title: Network-Level Organism Behavior Contract

rows.acceptance: Add governed proactive communication and night-cycle dream-processing loops that surface surprising, evidence-linked improvements while remaining within constitutional directive and veto boundaries.
rows.problem: The system will not feel alive unless it demonstrates persistent stylistic evolution and unprompted high-value insight surfacing.
rows.title: Alien-Feel Proactive Evolution Contract
